codeReview.summary: The feature implementation satisfies VAL-VOLUME-006 and VAL-VOLUME-007: MAX_PER_RUN is now 500, enrich_outreach.py replaced the sequential per-lead loop with a ThreadPoolExecutor-based parallel path capped by MAX_WORKERS=10, and the prior per-lead time.sleep(0.3) delay was removed. Error handling remains resilient at per-lead granularity, and the new tests cover cap value, no-sleep behavior, and parallelized execution path.

issues.description: The timing assertion for parallel behavior (elapsed < 1.5s with 10 leads and 0.05s mocked latency) is too loose to uniquely prove parallelism; a sequential implementation without sleep (~0.5s) would also pass.
issues.suggestedFix: Add a comparative runtime assertion using identical mocked latency with max_workers=1 versus max_workers=10, and assert a meaningful speedup ratio (e.g., parallel <= 60% of single-worker runtime).

issues.description: test_enrich_leads_parallel_returns_correct_messages only checks result count, so it does not validate lead-to-message correctness despite the test name/docstring implying message validation.
issues.suggestedFix: Assert that returned tuples contain expected lead IDs and corresponding generated message values (order-independent comparison).

summary: Reviewed async-outreach-enrichment commit c446997 and the worker transcript skeleton. The core feature is correctly implemented and meets the volume/parallel contract requirements; no blocking defects were found. Two non-blocking test-strength gaps were noted in tests/test_async_outreach.py around strict parallelism proof and message-mapping assertions.
